old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Controlled ceramic firing practice supplies the kiln and heat-control background for producing fired clay bricks.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica says potters at Ur discovered the principle of the closed kiln in which heat could be controlled, immediately after discussing burned brick production.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links fired-brick history to controlled kiln practice associated with potters at Ur.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is enabling, not a claim that pottery vessels are components of bricks; it records the shared ceramic firing and kiln-control background.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: pottery remains an enabling prerequisite for early kiln-fired bricks, now with a source-backed kiln rationale.
would_reject_if: The fired-brick node is later narrowed to unfired adobe or mud-brick architecture.
A stronger source shows early kiln-fired bricks developed without ceramic kiln or pottery firing practice.
The pottery node is narrowed to vessel form only and excludes kiln-firing knowledge.
short_reason: Fired bricks share controlled clay-firing and kiln practice with pottery.
